description: Regex patterns for static code analysis detection - ALL 10 LANGUAGES

vulnerabilities.message: SQL query may be vulnerable to injection attacks. Use parameterized queries.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use parameterized queries with SqlParameter or Entity Framework's parameterized methods.

vulnerabilities.message: OS command may be vulnerable to injection attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and sanitize user input before passing to system commands. Use allowlists when possible.

vulnerabilities.message: Endpoint may be vulnerable to reflected XSS attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Encode user input before rendering. Use Content() with proper encoding or HtmlEncoder.

vulnerabilities.message: I/O operation may be vulnerable to path injection attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate file paths and use Path.GetFullPath() to resolve paths before operations.

vulnerabilities.message: XML parser may be vulnerable to XXE attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Configure XmlReaderSettings with DtdProcessing.Prohibit and XmlResolver = null.

vulnerabilities.message: Weak cipher algorithm detected. Use AES with CBC or GCM mode.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use Aes.Create() with CipherMode.CBC or CipherMode.GCM.

vulnerabilities.message: Cryptographic key size is too small.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use at least 2048-bit keys for RSA and 256-bit for ECDSA.

vulnerabilities.message: Weak SSL/TLS protocol version detected.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use TLS 1.2 or TLS 1.3 only.

vulnerabilities.message: Password hashing is weak or uses insufficient iterations.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use a strong password hashing algorithm like Argon2, bcrypt, or PBKDF2 with at least 100,000 iterations.

vulnerabilities.message: HTTP redirect may be vulnerable to open redirect attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate redirect URLs against an allowlist of trusted domains.

securityHotspots.suggestion: Use secure configuration management like Azure Key Vault, environment variables, or user secrets.
Enable anti-forgery token validation for state-changing operations.
Restrict CORS to specific trusted origins.

securityHotspots.message: Debug features may be enabled in production.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Ensure debug features are only enabled in development environment.

securityHotspots.message: Pseudorandom number generator used. May not be suitable for security purposes.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Use RandomNumberGenerator for cryptographic purposes.

bugs.suggestion: Add null checks before accessing object members.

bugs.message: Async method returns void instead of Task.
bugs.suggestion: Change return type to Task to allow proper exception handling and await.

bugs.message: Object may be disposed more than once.
bugs.suggestion: Use 'using' statement or ensure Dispose is called only once.
This assignment has no effect. Remove or fix the assignment.

codeSmells.message: Method has too many parameters (more than 7).
codeSmells.suggestion: Consider using a parameter object or builder pattern.
Track this task in your issue tracker and remove the TODO comment when done.

vulnerabilities.message: SQL query may be vulnerable to injection attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements.

vulnerabilities.message: OS command may be vulnerable to injection attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate input and avoid shell execution. Use spawn with arguments array.

vulnerabilities.message: DOM update may lead to XSS attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use textContent instead of innerHTML, or sanitize input with DOMPurify.

vulnerabilities.message: File operation may be vulnerable to path traversal.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and sanitize file paths. Use path.resolve and check against base directory.

vulnerabilities.message: NoSQL query may be vulnerable to injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate input types and use MongoDB's query sanitization.

securityHotspots.suggestion: Use environment variables or a secrets manager.
Restrict CORS to specific trusted origins.
Enable CSRF protection for state-changing operations.

bugs.message: NaN comparison will always be false.
bugs.suggestion: Use Number.isNaN() instead of comparing with NaN.

bugs.message: Array.sort() without compare function may produce unexpected results.
bugs.suggestion: Provide a compare function for consistent sorting.
This assignment has no effect.

react.message: Using array index as key is not recommended.
react.suggestion: Use a unique identifier from your data as the key.
Hooks must be called in the same order on every render. Move them outside conditionals.
Add all reactive values used inside the effect to the dependency array.

vulnerabilities.message: SQL query may be vulnerable to injection attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use parameterized queries with placeholders.

vulnerabilities.message: OS command may be vulnerable to injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Avoid shell=True. Use list of arguments instead of string.

vulnerabilities.message: Deserialization of untrusted data detected.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use yaml.safe_load() or json instead of unsafe deserialization.

vulnerabilities.message: File operation may be vulnerable to path traversal.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and sanitize file paths. Use os.path.realpath and check against base directory.

securityHotspots.suggestion: Use environment variables or a secrets manager.

securityHotspots.message: Debug mode may be enabled in production.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Disable debug mode in production environments.
Use SHA-256 or stronger for security-sensitive operations.

bugs.suggestion: This assignment has no effect.
Check for zero before division.

vulnerabilities.message: SQL query may be vulnerable to injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use parameterized queries with placeholders.

vulnerabilities.message: OS command may be vulnerable to injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and sanitize command arguments.

vulnerabilities.message: TLS certificate verification is disabled.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Enable TLS certificate verification in production.

vulnerabilities.message: File operation may be vulnerable to path traversal.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and sanitize file paths.

securityHotspots.suggestion: Use environment variables or a secrets manager.
Use SHA-256 or stronger for security-sensitive operations.

bugs.suggestion: This assignment has no effect.

bugs.message: HTTP response body may not be closed.
bugs.suggestion: Always close the response body with defer resp.Body.Close().

vulnerabilities.message: SQL query may be vulnerable to injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use PreparedStatement with parameterized queries.

vulnerabilities.message: OS command may be vulnerable to injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and sanitize command arguments.

vulnerabilities.message: XML parser may be vulnerable to XXE attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Configure XML parser to disable DTD and external entities.

vulnerabilities.message: Deserialization of untrusted data detected.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use allowlists for deserialization or avoid deserializing untrusted data.

vulnerabilities.message: Endpoint may be vulnerable to XSS.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Encode user input before output.

securityHotspots.suggestion: Use environment variables or a secrets manager.
Use SHA-256 or stronger.
Enable CSRF protection for state-changing operations.

bugs.suggestion: Add null checks before method calls.
This assignment has no effect.

vulnerabilities.message: Unsafe unwrap/expect detected. Use proper error handling with match or ? operator.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Replace unwrap with match or use ? operator for error propagation.

vulnerabilities.message: Unsafe block detected. Ensure memory safety guarantees.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Minimize unsafe blocks and document safety invariants.

vulnerabilities.message: Raw pointer usage detected.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Prefer safe abstractions over raw pointers.

vulnerabilities.message: Transmute is highly unsafe and should be avoided.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use safe conversion methods or From/Into traits.

vulnerabilities.message: Potential integer overflow handling.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use checked arithmetic or ensure values cannot overflow.

vulnerabilities.message: OS command may be vulnerable to injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Pass command arguments as separate parameters, not formatted strings.

vulnerabilities.message: SQL query may be vulnerable to injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use parameterized queries with placeholders.

securityHotspots.suggestion: Use environment variables or secure vaults for secrets.

securityHotspots.message: Pseudorandom number generator used. May not be suitable for security purposes.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Use cryptographically secure random number generators for security purposes.

bugs.suggestion: Ensure loop has a clear exit condition.
Add base case to prevent infinite recursion.
This assignment has no effect.

codeSmells.message: Functions should not return unit type explicitly.

codeSmells.suggestion: Address the technical debt before merging.

vulnerabilities.message: Potential SQL injection. Use prepared statements with parameter binding.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use prepared statements with bindParam() or bindValue().

vulnerabilities.message: Potential XSS vulnerability. User input may not be escaped.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use htmlspecialchars() when outputting user input.

vulnerabilities.message: OS command may be vulnerable to injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and escape user input before passing to system commands.

vulnerabilities.message: File operation may be vulnerable to path traversal.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate file paths against allowed directories.

vulnerabilities.message: Deserialization of untrusted data detected.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Avoid deserializing untrusted data or use allowlists.

securityHotspots.suggestion: Use environment variables or a secrets manager.
Use password_hash() with PASSWORD_DEFAULT or SHA-256+.

bugs.message: Potential division by zero or type juggling issue.
bugs.suggestion: Validate divisor before division.
This assignment has no effect.

codeSmells.message: Use of die() or exit() found.
codeSmells.suggestion: Use exceptions for error handling instead of die()/exit().

vulnerabilities.message: Potential SQL injection. Use parameterized queries or Room.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use Room's @Query annotation with parameters or PreparedStatement.

vulnerabilities.message: File operation may be vulnerable to path traversal.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate file paths and use canonical paths.

vulnerabilities.message: Deserialization of untrusted data detected.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Avoid deserializing untrusted data or use serialization allowlists.

vulnerabilities.message: OS command execution detected.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and sanitize command arguments.

securityHotspots.suggestion: Use BuildConfig fields from environment or Android Keystore.
Use SHA-256 or stronger.
Use HTTPS for network communications.

bugs.message: Null safety bypass or potential NPE.
bugs.suggestion: Use proper null handling with ?. or ?: instead of !!
This assignment has no effect.

vulnerabilities.message: Potential SQL injection. Use parameterized queries.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use ActiveRecord's parameterized methods or bind parameters.

vulnerabilities.message: OS command may be vulnerable to injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and sanitize user input before passing to system commands.

vulnerabilities.message: Deserialization of untrusted data detected.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use safe loading methods or validate input before deserialization.

vulnerabilities.message: Potential XSS vulnerability.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use html_escape() or Rails' built-in escaping. Avoid html_safe.

vulnerabilities.message: File operation may be vulnerable to path traversal.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate file paths against allowed directories.

securityHotspots.message: Possible hard-coded credentials or fallback detected.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Use environment variables without fallbacks for secrets.
Use SHA-256 or stronger. For passwords, use bcrypt or Argon2.
Ensure debug features are only enabled in development.

bugs.suggestion: This assignment has no effect.
Check for zero before division.

codeSmells.suggestion: Track this task in your issue tracker.

vulnerabilities.message: Potential buffer overflow. Use safer alternatives like strncpy, strncat, snprintf.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Replace with bounded versions: strncpy, strncat, snprintf, or use std::string.

vulnerabilities.message: Using clear-text protocols is security-sensitive.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use HTTPS instead of HTTP.

vulnerabilities.message: Hard-coded credentials are security-sensitive.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use environment variables or a secure credential store.

vulnerabilities.message: Using weak random number generators is security-sensitive.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use cryptographically secure random number generators.

vulnerabilities.message: I/O operation may be vulnerable to path injection attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and sanitize file paths before use.

vulnerabilities.message: Potential format string vulnerability or XSS.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use parameterized output or sanitize user input.

vulnerabilities.message: Database query may be vulnerable to SQL injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements.

vulnerabilities.message: Memory operations should be bounded.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Ensure size parameters are properly validated.

securityHotspots.message: Using weak hashing algorithms is security-sensitive.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Use stronger hashing algorithms like SHA-256 or bcrypt.
Ensure proper authentication and encryption.
Validate archive contents before extraction.

securityHotspots.message: Using dynamically formatted SQL queries is security-sensitive.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Use parameterized queries.

bugs.suggestion: Check allocation size and handle failures.

bugs.message: Memory allocated but potentially not freed.
bugs.suggestion: Ensure memory is freed or use smart pointers.
Check for null before dereferencing.
Use delete[] for arrays, delete for single objects.
Avoid returning pointers to stack variables.
Review the comparison logic.

bugs.message: Switch case may be missing break.
bugs.suggestion: Ensure each case ends with break or return.

codeSmells.message: Magic numbers should not be used.

codeSmells.suggestion: Consider grouping parameters into a struct.

codeSmells.message: Header file should use include guards.
codeSmells.suggestion: Add proper include guards to header files.

codeSmells.message: Control structures should use curly braces.
codeSmells.suggestion: Always use braces even for single-line blocks.

codeSmells.message: Unused local variables should be removed.

codeSmells.suggestion: Track this task in your issue tracker.
